summary: Good‑quality soundboard matrix (dry, flat image) captures a mixed July ’78 Red Rocks show with standout Cassidy, Dancing in the Street and Black Peter.
review: The recording is a soundboard/matrix source that sounds clear but somewhat dry and flat, yet reviewers still rate it highly for fidelity. Musically the concert is considered a lesser‑than‑stellar night on the tour, though highlights like Cassidy, Dancing in the Street and a superb Black Peter keep it interesting. Overall sentiment is mixed because of strong sound praise against an average performance assessment.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality averages around 3¼ stars while recording quality receives high marks; weighted average yields ~3.8.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Original SBD was mono; a matrix adds stereo image but sounds dry and flat, typical of the ’78 tour. Song titles are mis‑labelled from "Space" onward, which is a metadata issue rather than audio quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rated B‑, second set C+, overall 3¼ stars; first half of the show is tighter than the second, with a few average or sloppy moments but strong peaks in the middle.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Warms up and takes off with Jack Straw; no specific criticism.
band_member_comments.Phil: "Phil is the man on Tennessee" – bass work highlighted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith excels on Big River, though his parts are only clear in certain audio mixes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums Not Fade described as average with a brief Nobody's Fault quote.
